people.name: Marc Rico-Pastó
people.summary: Marc Rico-Pastó is a postdoctoral researcher working at the interface of physics and biomedicine. His research focuses on single-molecule biophysics, feedback thermodynamics, and mechanobiology applied to cancer research. He studies the mechanical properties of lung cancer cells using force-spectroscopy techniques to understand tumor progression and metastasis. His previous work explored molecular folding, fluctuation theorems, and non-equilibrium thermodynamics through experimental and theoretical approaches.

people.name: Victoria Batto
people.area: Tumor Microenvironment, Immunology, Lung Cancer Research
people.summary: Victoria Batto is a graduate researcher working on translational cancer research focused on the tumor microenvironment and the immunosuppressive role of tumor-associated fibroblasts in lung cancer. She combines experience from the pharmaceutical industry with strong training in molecular and cellular biology techniques, including immunology, virology, and functional cell assays.

people.name: Teia Vallès
people.area: Cancer Biology, Breast Cancer Metastasis, Tumor Microenvironment
people.summary: Teia Vallès is a predoctoral researcher focused on cancer biology and metastasis. Her research investigates how tumor-associated fibroblasts regulate invasion, migration, and survival of cancer cells, particularly in HER2+ breast cancer. She has research experience in international cancer laboratories and expertise in cell culture, organ-on-chip models, confocal microscopy, and molecular analysis techniques.

people.name: Laura López
people.summary: Pathologist associated with the lab, collaborating on translational studies and tissue-based characterization.

people.name: José Rodríguez
people.summary: Medical image computing engineer specializing in quantitative medical imaging, AI-driven analysis, and research software development. His work focuses on building image processing pipelines for biomedical applications, including histopathology segmentation, registration and feature extraction. He combines experience in medical device R&D, regulatory environments, and interdisciplinary collaboration to translate computational methods into practical healthcare solutions.

education.degree: M.Sc. in Design and Management of Technological Projects

people.name: Nanthilde Malandain
people.summary: Collaborates on tissue nanomechanics and mechanobiology questions, linking physical measurements with biology.
